preview_text:
The model favors Ecuador with an 82.6% win probability, leaving Curaçao a slim 1.1% chance of victory. This represents a decisive gap between the two sides in the statistical forecast.

The spread reflects a match heavily tilted toward an Ecuador victory, though the 16.3% draw probability suggests some realistic path to a stalemate. The model assigns minimal likelihood to a Curaçao win, indicating the match is expected to be competitive only in whether Ecuador controls the result or allows a draw; an upset appears highly improbable under this view.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge modestly on the outcome probabilities. Sportsbooks grant Ecuador 79.0% and Curaçao 7.0%, meaning they are notably more bullish on an underdog result than the model's 1.1% figure. Polymarket prices align more closely with the model on Ecuador (79.9%) but also lean toward a higher Curaçao probability (6.7%) than the statistical forecast. These differences suggest market participants assign more tail risk to an upset than the model does.
